name: Dolmas with Lamb
recipeIngredient: 1 Jar (16 oz) grape leaves, packed in brine
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash and drain the rice.
2. Heat 1 cup olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.
3. Add the yellow onion and sauté until soft but not brown, about 5 minutes.
4. Add ground lamb and fry with onion until done.
5. Transfer to a mixing bowl and add the drained rice, 1/2 cup of the remaining olive oil, green onions, parsley, mint, pine nuts, cinnamon, allspice, salt, and pepper.
6. Rinse grape leaves under running cold water to remove as much brine as possible; pat dry and stack on a plate.
7. Place one leaf at a time, shiny side down, on a flat work surface. Cut off and discard the tough stem end.
8. Spoon about 1 tablespoon of the rice mixture in the center near the base of the leaf.
9. Fold the stem end over to cover the filling; fold both sides inward, lengthwise, and then tightly roll leaf toward pointed tip end to form a compact packet.
10. Repeat with remaining leaves and filling.
11. Pour about 2 tablespoons of the remaining olive oil in the bottom of a large pot and scatter with parsley stems to prevent grape leaves from sticking.
12. Arrange the stuffed leaves, seam side down, almost touching on top of the parsley, making as many layers as necessary.
13. Drizzle the remaining 6 tablespoons olive oil, lemon juice, and 1/2 cup broth over the leaves.
14. Top with a heat-resistant plate and weight with a heavy can to keep leaves from unwinding during cooking.
15. Cover the pot, bring to a gentle boil, reduce heat to low, and cook until rice is tender about 1 hour.
16. During cooking, add a little heated liquid as needed to keep dolmas moist.
17. Remove from heat and cool in the pot.
18. Sprinkle with lemon juice to taste, garnish with lemon zest, and serve at room temperature.
